entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION OPTIMIZATION
entities.description: The process of maximizing crop yields through efficient resource use

entities.name: SOIL MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of maintaining soil health and fertility

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: Farming practices that meet current needs without compromising future generations

entities.name: TILLAGE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The strategic manipulation of soil to create favorable conditions for crop growth

entities.name: SOIL CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: The physical, chemical, and biological properties of soil

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FARMING
entities.description: Agricultural production that is environmentally sound and economically viable

entities.name: SOIL RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of a soil ecosystem to recover from disturbances

entities.name: REDUCED TILLAGE
entities.description: Tillage practices that minimize soil disturbance

entities.name: MARESHA TILLAGE
entities.description: A traditional tillage system used in Ethiopia

entities.name: BULK DENSITY
entities.description: The mass of dry soil per unit volume

entities.name: SOIL COMPACTION
entities.description: The compression of soil particles, reducing pore space

entities.name: ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The carbon component of soil organic matter

entities.name: PENETRATION RATES
entities.description: The speed at which water or roots penetrate the soil

entities.name: MOISTURE RETENTION
entities.description: The ability of soil to hold water

entities.name: SOIL LOSS
entities.description: The removal of the topsoil layer by erosion

entities.name: DEEP TILLAGE
entities.description: Tillage that reaches deeper soil layers to break hardpans

entities.name: DETERIORATED SOILS
entities.description: Soils that have lost their productivity or structure

entities.name: WATER DYNAMICS
entities.description: The movement and distribution of water within the soil

entities.name: HYDRAULIC CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: The ease with which water can move through soil pores

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The ratio of agricultural outputs to inputs

entities.name: WATER PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit of water used

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEM METHODS
entities.description: The arrangement of crops in time and space

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The organic component of soil, including plant and animal residues

entities.name: SOIL DISTURBANCE
entities.description: The physical disruption of soil structure

entities.name: CROP RESIDUE
entities.description: Plant material left in the field after harvest

entities.name: CARBON SINK
entities.description: A reservoir that absorbs more carbon than it releases

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the study is focused

entities.name: CLIMATE ADAPTABILITY
entities.description: The ability of agricultural systems to adjust to climate change

entities.name: WATER SECURITY
entities.description: The reliable availability of an acceptable quantity and quality of water

entities.name: SOIL RESTORATION
entities.description: The process of returning degraded soil to its original state

entities.name: SOIL ECOSYSTEM
entities.description: The community of organisms and their environment within the soil

entities.name: WATER RESOURCES
entities.description: Sources of water that are potentially useful
